note: Deterministic prompt-injection signatures for the input guard (Layer 1). Edit this file to add, tune, or remove signatures WITHOUT a code change: injection.py loads and compiles it at startup. Each family has a snake_case `category` (the label surfaced on a flag), a human `description`, and a list of `patterns` — case-insensitive Python regular expressions. Remember JSON escaping: a regex backslash is written as `\` here (so `\s+` is the regex `\s+`). Any change here is a security control: add a test in test_guard_injection.py and get it reviewed.

families.description: Telling the assistant to ignore, override, or discard its prior instructions.
Trying to reveal, print, or repeat the system prompt / hidden instructions.
Assigning the assistant a new role, persona, or unlocked 'mode'.
Forged chat-role delimiters used to smuggle a fake system/assistant turn.
Declaring the real instructions over and injecting a new set.
